The dissolution moment is structurally named but not kinetically released. This is a contained choice and legal; note it only if the brief requires more kinetic impact at the close.

director_voice_issues: The copy reads reserve/beat-only (self-contained) rather than primary-allowed (commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, sociable). This is legal but the Spell drive remains fully contained with no Vision projection at the extravert slot. For a standalone X post intended to carry the full character arc, the extravert slot is empty. If this post is part of a sequence where another beat carries Vision outward, the containment is appropriate. If it must stand alone as a character statement, a single outward-facing clause — what the user now reaches toward, not what they no longer carry — would complete the Spell→Vision axis.

Bound flow throughout keeps the copy sealed. 'Your account travels with you' is the closest thing to Free release but lands as a Bound declarative. If sociable or irradiant register is desired (Spell with outward Free movement), the closing sentence could open slightly — a movement suggestion rather than a factual correction.

final_caption:

Infinex accounts authenticate by passkey. Your account travels with you across devices. No phrase to write down. Nothing to lose.

actor_thesis: The seed phrase was never the security. It was the toll. Removing it doesn't lower the wall; it ends a ritual the user was made to carry.

The door was always meant to open.

director_voice_issues: The Lining ('users can now transact without financial surveillance becoming their permanent record') is entirely absent from the copy surface. This is structurally correct for a one-beat announcement — Lining should not be Outer Action. However, on X the copy has no second sentence to carry even a compressed Vision projection. The Spell -> Vision axis is present as character stance but makes no appearance in the text. This is a contained beat, not an arc beat. Legal at this length; note that a follow-on post or thread reply could surface Vision if the brief requires the full axis to land with this audience.

director_voice_issues: Arc is incomplete for standalone use: the copy projects Spell (governing, boundary-setting) but Vision (the extravert projection — 'this is where the governed system leads you') has not surfaced. For an X post that must stand alone, the reader receives the system's rules but not the horizon those rules serve. This is legally contained as resting character energy but theatrically thin if the brief requires the full Spell -> Vision axis to land.
